description: A steady hand follows a line without wavering; a steady wind keeps roughly the same force. Both are 'kolu' because their course varies little over time. The pace may be fast or slow. 'mureo' (stable) instead describes a state that survives disturbance.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k' and release into rounded mid-back 'o'. The tongue tip then touches the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' before rounded high-back 'u'. Stress falls on 'ko'.
sound_symbolism: The firm release of 'k' starts the word cleanly. Lateral 'l' then carries the second syllable without a jolt, and the two rounded vowels keep the spoken course even.
usage_notes: 'kolu howeli' is a steady wind. Pace and pressure can be steady when their variation stays small. So can a repeated action.

semantic_domains.physical: motion or pressure that varies little while continuing
